findings.description: The PDF memo header uses a clear two-column layout with 'To:', 'From:', 'Subject:' labels on the left and values indented on the right. The HTML collapses this into a single-column run-on format (e.g., 'To: Donald R. van der Vaart, Secretary' on one line).
findings.evidence: PDF shows formatted memo fields with label-value alignment; HTML renders them as simple prefixed lines without the tabular alignment structure.

findings.description: The digital signature block appears at the top of the HTML conversion between the header and the memo body, which is not present in the visible PDF rendering.
findings.evidence: HTML shows 'Digitally signed by LYF DN: cn=LYF, o=DWM, ou=SWS, email=larry.frost@ncdenr.gov, c=US Date: 2016.05.12 12:11:52 -04'00'' at top of content. This is not visible on the PDF page 1.

findings.description: In the PDF, the outline items (I through IV) use Roman numerals with clear indentation as a formatted list. The HTML renders them similarly but with slightly different list formatting that is still readable.
findings.evidence: Both versions show the same four outline items with Roman numerals. The HTML uses a slightly different indentation style but content is preserved.

findings.description: The written public comments section in the PDF (page 4) lists topics including 'Off-site' and 'Other' bullet points that are missing from the HTML version of the written comments summary.
findings.evidence: PDF page 4 written comments summary has bullets for Health Issues, Ash Basin Classification, Groundwater, Surface Water, Dam Safety, Property Value, and Landfills. The HTML written comments section matches these same bullets without Off-site or Other (which are correctly only in the oral comments section).
